name: GolfDojo
address: 7350 Transit Rd Rear Suite, Buffalo, NY 14221
about: GolfDojo operates multiple indoor golf simulation centers in Western New York, featuring a high-tech environment for golf practice and play. Their facilities use TrackMan dual radar technology, providing accurate ball and club tracking data for a realistic on-course experience. The venues offer public bookings from 10am to 10pm daily, with members receiving 24/7 access to the facilities. In addition to simulator bays, GolfDojo provides professional coaching through its academy and hosts leagues and events for various skill levels.
why_golfers_like_it: Premium TrackMan dual radar technology for accurate shot data and simulation
Flexible booking with both public hours and 24/7 member access
Professional coaching available from PGA professionals
Multiple locations throughout Western New York for convenience

food_and_drink.notes: Food service appears to be available based on imagery showing tables with pizza
BYOB not mentioned, with the one 1812 Tavern location being a separate establishment

simulator_hardware.notes: Used across all locations for high-fidelity ball and club tracking

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't explicitly state whether clubs are provided. It is recommended to bring your own equipment for the most accurate fitting and practice experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Food service appears to be available at the venues based on imagery. The GolfDojo @ 1812 Tavern location is part of a restaurant, while other locations may have food options. BYOB policy is not clearly stated.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the time required to complete 18 holes. Typical indoor golf simulator rounds can take 1-2 hours depending on the number of players and pace of play.

faq.question: Is GolfDojo kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, GolfDojo offers junior lesson packages and hosts high school golf clinics, indicating they cater to younger golfers and families.
